/ 10, 1), 20)\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(step_idx: int) -> float:\\n        current_step = step_idx + 1\\n        if current_step <= warmup_steps:\\n            return current_step / warmup_steps\\n        progress = (current_step - warmup_steps) / max(total_steps - warmup_steps, 1)\\n        cosine = 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n        return 0.1 + 0.9 * cosine\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"Use the fixed-step budget more effectively by pairing AdamW with a substantially larger learning rate and a short warmup plus cosine decay targeted to the actual training horizon.
